skill.name: common-pitfalls
skill.description: Coordinates code review checks for common pitfalls across frontend, backend, database, blockchain, WebSocket, and security work.
skill.summary: Guides code reviews through common production issue categories and targeted prevention checklists.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only markdown inline code, glob examples, and ordinary checklist text. No executable scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings at lines 25-31, 66, and 79 are markdown inline code and file pattern examples. They are review guidance, not shell execution or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are inside markdown tables and checklist items. There is no script file, command invocation, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at lines 3, 47, and 59 do not reference weak algorithms. Line 3 describes the skill, line 47 names Express API pitfalls, and line 59 checks API status codes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain documentation text. The only cryptography mention is a separate recommendation for AES-256-GCM on line 83, which is not a weak algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Static Pattern: Backtick Syntax in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: Backticks appear only as markdown formatting around terms such as file globs, TypeScript names, and allowFailure. They do not execute commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is located in documentation tables and checklists, not source code. No command runner or shell context exists in the scanned file.

content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss recurring issues across frameworks, APIs, databases, and security boundaries. This skill organizes checks by code category and produces a combined review checklist.
actual_capabilities: Identifies review categories from changed file patterns and code terms.
Maps frontend, backend, database, WebSocket, blockchain, and security areas to focused pitfall checks.
Provides a quick checklist for query keys, mutations, schema types, API routes, RPC calls, and loading states.
Includes financial and blockchain review reminders for BigInt, Decimal.js, gas handling, and transaction simulation.
Highlights security review reminders for secrets, session keys, audit logs, and rate limits.
limitations: It is an orchestration guide and does not include the referenced sub-skill implementations.
It does not run automated tests, static analysis, or dependency scans by itself.
It depends on the reviewer to inspect changed files and apply relevant checklist items.
It cannot guarantee production safety without project-specific review and verification.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to classify changed files and check common defects before merging.

use_cases.title: Audit Unfamiliar Code
use_cases.description: Use category prompts to inspect React, Express, database, WebSocket, blockchain, and security patterns quickly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the combined checklist to find recurring production risks before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Pitfall Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changed files with the common-pitfalls checklist. Identify matching categories and list the most important issues to verify.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Frontend Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused React and TanStack Query review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use common-pitfalls to review this frontend change. Focus on query keys, invalidation, loading states, error states, accessibility, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review Backend and Data Paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Use common-pitfalls to inspect these backend changes. Focus on Express routes, status codes, Drizzle types, migrations, validation, and storage patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Perform a Cross-Domain Risk Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply common-pitfalls across this full change set. Categorize risks, combine findings, rank severity, and call out required tests or manual checks.

output_examples.input: A pull request modifies React components and TanStack Query hooks.
output_examples.output: The review flags frontend and query categories. It checks full query keys, mutation invalidation, loading states, error states, accessibility, and responsive behavior.

output_examples.input: A backend change updates Express routes and Drizzle schema files.
output_examples.output: The review focuses on route status codes, schema-derived types, migrations, runtime validation, and storage consistency.

output_examples.input: A release includes blockchain RPC calls and session handling changes.
output_examples.output: The review highlights RPC error handling, gas estimation, transaction simulation, session expiry, secret handling, audit logs, and rate limits.

best_practices: Start by mapping changed files to the listed review categories.
Record concrete findings with file references and verification steps.
Pair checklist findings with tests, manual checks, or follow-up tickets.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the checklist as proof that code is safe.
Do not apply every category when the changed files do not match.
Do not ignore project-specific risks outside the listed categories.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It organizes code review checks around common development pitfalls and helps combine findings across several technical areas.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown checklist and workflow guide. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which technologies does it mention?
faq.answer: It mentions TanStack Query, Drizzle ORM, Express API, React, WebSocket, blockchain RPC, and security review areas.

faq.question: Can it replace automated tests?
faq.answer: No. It supports review planning, but automated tests and project-specific validation are still required.

faq.question: Why were security findings dismissed?
faq.answer: The flagged patterns were markdown formatting and ordinary documentation text, not executable commands or weak cryptography usage.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Engineers, technical leads, and release reviewers can use it to structure multi-area code reviews.
